text: This is usually rendered, ‘he will take up his shield against an enemy’, but this, without further qualification, seems meaningless.
The words ‘if thou wilt’ are not in the original; but the context evidently demands them in the Latin version; the Greek provides no difficulty; since it gives a negative, ‘Do not consult …’.
The text here is uncertain, and the meaning obscure. The Latin seems to demand some such rendering as that given above.
